zh.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne是Baron Walter de Chesney和Eve de Broc的女兒，出生在英國盧特藍德格拉爾。她於1169年之前與Almaric de Spencer在英國結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1198年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne是Baron Walter de Chesney和Eve de Broc的女儿，出生在英国盧特藍德格拉爾。她于1169年之前与Almaric de Spencer在英国结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1198年在英国英格兰貝德福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cheyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (northeastern Scotland and Shetland): shortened form of   Chaney  , of Norman origin.

variants.name: Chaney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any one of several places in Normandy, Picardy, or Artois (France) named (Le) Quesnay or Quesnoy, or elsewhere in northern France named (Le or La) Chesnay or Chesnoy. The placenames all derive from Old French chesnai or its Normanno-Picard equivalent quesnai ‘oak grove’ (medieval Latin casnetum).
French: variant of Chanet, a habitational name from any of various places called Chanet or Le Chanet, from Latin canna ‘reed’ + the suffix -etum denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Amabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin amabilis ‘lovable’ via Old French. Although now very rare in the English-speaking world, this name lies behind the much commoner name Annabel and also Mabel . It gained some currency from being borne by the character Amabel Rose Adams in Angela Thirkell's Barsetshire Chronicles ( 1933 onwards).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne was born in Ryhall, Rutland, England as the daughter of Baron Walter de Chesney and Eve de Broc. She married Almaric de Spencer before 1169, in England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1198, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne est née à Ryhall, Rutland, Angleterre, fille de Baron Walter de Chesney et Eve de Broc. Elle a épousé Almaric de Spencer avant 1169, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1198, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne wurde in Ryhall, Rutland, England, als Tochter von Baron Walter de Chesney und Eve de Broc geboren. Sie hat vor 1169 in England Almaric de Spencer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1198 in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne è nata il nel Ryhall, Rutland, England come figlia di Baron Walter de Chesney e Eve de Broc. Lei sposato Almaric de Spencer prima del 1169, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1198, nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de CheyneはイギリスルトランドリハルでBaron Walter de ChesneyとEve de Brocの娘として生まれた。1169年以前にイギリスでAlmaric de Spencerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1198年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne(은)는 영국 루틀랜드 리홀에서 Baron Walter de Chesney(와)과 Eve de Broc의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1169년 이전 영국에서 Almaric de Spencer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1198년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne nasceu em Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra filha de Baron Walter de Chesney e Eve de Broc. Ela casou-se com Almaric de Spencer antes 1169, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1198, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne родилась в местности рихолл, рутланд, Англия. Её родителей звали Baron Walter de Chesney и Eve de Broc. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Almaric de Spencer перед 1169 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1198 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Amabel Maud de Cheyne nació en Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra como hija de Baron Walter de Chesney y Eve de Broc. Se casó con Almaric de Spencer antes de 1169, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1198, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
